source: dart analyze, Effective Dart, Flutter best practices

rules.name: console-output
rules.message: print()/debugPrint() — use the logging package for structured logging.

rules.name: hardcoded-credentials
rules.fix_hint: Move secrets to environment variables, dart-define, or a secure storage solution.

rules.name: null-assertion-abuse
rules.message: Null assertion (!) — throws at runtime if value is null.
rules.fix_hint: Use null-aware operators (?., ??, ??=) or explicit null checks instead.

rules.name: dynamic-type
rules.message: dynamic type disables type checking — use Object? or a specific type.
rules.fix_hint: Replace with a typed parameter, Object?, or generic type parameter.

rules.name: catch-error
rules.message: Catching Error — Error represents programming bugs, not recoverable conditions.
rules.fix_hint: Catch specific Exception subtypes; let Errors propagate to crash reporting.

rules.name: sync-io
rules.message: Synchronous I/O — blocks the event loop. Use async variants.
rules.fix_hint: Replace with the async version (readAsString, writeAsString, etc.).

rules.name: deep-nesting
rules.message: Deep nesting detected (4+ indent levels with 2-space indent) — reduces readability.
rules.fix_hint: Extract inner logic into named functions or use early returns.

rules.name: magic-numbers
rules.message: Magic number in comparison — extract to a named constant for clarity.
rules.fix_hint: Define a const or final variable with a descriptive name.

rules.name: empty-catch
rules.message: Empty catch block silently swallows exceptions.
rules.fix_hint: Log the error, rethrow, or add a comment explaining why it is intentionally ignored.
